event: acute febrile illness with undifferentiated fever (provisional diagnosis)
liver function test showed mild hyperbilirubinemia with transaminitis
ultrasound of the abdomen showed mild fatty infiltration of the liver
treated with tablet doxycycline 100 mg twice daily
deteriorated neurologically on treatment with doxycycline
shifted to the Intensive Care Unit
MRI of the brain without any abnormality
MRI of the cervical spine revealed no abnormality
nerve conduction velocity showed motor sensory demyelinating polyneuropathy
cerebrospinal fluid analysis did not reveal albuminocytologic dissociation
CSF cell count: 70 with lymphocytic predominance
intravenous immunoglobulin therapy 0.4 mg/kg/day for 5 days started
tablet rifampicin 600 mg twice daily added
serial liver function tests monitored within normal range
improvement in weakness of all four limbs
followed up regularly in the outpatient department
